fields.summary: Change ContainerExecutor's primary lifecycle methods to use a more extensible mechanism for passing information.
ContainerExecutor interface methods are too brittle

status.description: The issue is open and ready for the assignee to start work on it.
status.name: Open

issuetype.description: A problem which impairs or prevents the functions of the product.
issuetype.name: Bug

fields.description: As per description in parent JIRA :  Adding additional arguments to key ContainerExecutor methods ( e.g startLocalizer or launchContainer ) would break the existing ContainerExecutor interface and would require changes to all executor implementations in YARN. In order to make this interface less brittle in the future, it would make sense to encapsulate arguments in some kind of a ‘context’ object which could be modified/extended without breaking the ContainerExecutor interface in the future.

resolution.description: A fix for this issue is checked into the tree and tested.
resolution.name: Fixed

fields.summary: Container recovery is broken with delegating container runtime

status.description: A resolution has been taken, and it is awaiting verification by reporter. From here issues are either reopened, or are closed.
status.name: Resolved

comments.body: Uploading patch - modifying key methods to use use context objects instead of an argument list. This is more extensible and less brittle.
